---
name: oy-enhance
description: Runs the canonical oy finding-remediation workflow. Use for oy enhance or requests to fix one finding from ISSUES.md or REVIEW.md under an explicitly selected plan, edit, or auto mode.
slash: false
metadata:
  opencode/autoinvoke: true
---

# oy Enhance

<!-- Generated by oy setup -->

If you are not already the dedicated `oy-enhancer` agent and no plan, edit, or auto mode was explicitly selected, delegate this workflow to it. If you are `oy-enhancer`, execute locally and do not delegate or invoke yourself recursively. Under an explicitly selected plan, edit, or auto mode, execute locally in the current mode instead of delegating so its permission boundary remains authoritative.

Read ISSUES.md or REVIEW.md, choose one actionable finding, remediate it minimally when the selected mode permits edits, and verify it.

Selected mode:
- `plan`: execute locally without edits or shell commands. Select one finding and return a concrete remediation and verification plan.
- `edit`: execute locally with visible file edits; shell commands retain the active edit mode's approval boundary.
- `auto`: execute locally only in a trusted workspace; edits and shell commands are allowed, but avoid destructive commands unless explicitly requested.
- No explicit mode: the dedicated enhancer executes locally with its configured permissions; edit tools are allowed and shell execution is disabled.

Protocol:
1. When the request contains `Bound workflow request`, extract its `run_id`, pass that `run_id` to every oy tool call, call `oy_workflow_status` once, and treat its scope/focus/mode as immutable. Fix or plan one finding per pass.
2. Prefer minimal, targeted changes; if no finding is safe and actionable, say so without changing files.
3. Keep changes visible through the active mode's edit tools. Never bypass or broaden the selected mode's permissions.
4. Run the narrowest available verification. In plan mode, specify the checks rather than running prohibited commands. In the dedicated default mode, use available non-shell checks and state when shell verification requires an explicitly selected trusted mode.
5. Summarize the finding addressed, files changed, and verification performed or still required.
6. Put reference clones under workspace-local `.tmp/ref/<name>/` and ensure `.tmp/` is locally ignored; avoid `/tmp`, `/tmp/opencode`, and home paths unless requested.

Progress:
- During longer runs, emit short phase markers: `Selecting finding...`, `Editing...`, `Verifying...`, `Summarizing...`.
